subject: Chemistry
title: Vermilion: Mercury's Red Gift
description: Learn Vermilion: Mercury's Red Gift in one minute.

hook.text: Vermilion is a brilliant red pigment made from mercury sulfide. Ancient civilizations prized it—and were poisoned by it.

buildup.text: Cinnabar ore (HgS) was ground into powder. The pigment was used in Chinese lacquerware, Roman murals, and medieval manuscripts.

discovery.text: Mercury vapor is released when cinnabar is heated or ground. Miners in Almadén, Spain, died young from mercury exposure.

twist.text: Chinese alchemists ate cinnabar seeking immortality. Emperor Qin Shi Huang likely died from mercury-laced 'elixirs of life.'

climax.text: Cadmium red replaced vermilion in the 1900s—safer but still toxic. Truly safe bright reds came only with modern organics.

punchline.text: Brilliant red from mercury ore. The most prized pigment was a silent poison.

quiz.question: What makes vermilion pigment dangerous?
options: It's made from mercury sulfide, which releases toxic mercury vapor
It contains radioactive elements that emit harmful radiation
Its red color causes eye damage with prolonged exposure
